description: Perform recurring care or adjustment that keeps something near its required condition or function. Maintenance happens before and between failures. 'shiroka' (repair) answers damage or lost function after it occurs.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip lightly against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th' and let air pass into high-front 'i'. Make a tap or short trill at the alveolar ridge before stressed open central 'a'. Return the tongue tip to the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n' and finish through the nose into rounded high-back 'u'.
sound_symbolism: Breath, a brief tap, and a final nasal contact give the tongue three measured tasks. The recurring touches recall work done again and again.
usage_notes: The thing under upkeep is the object. A condition, interval, or standard can accompany it. 'thenoki' (sustain) supplies continuing support, while 'nelore' (preserve) protects something from loss or unwanted alteration.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Maintenance keeps materials and prior labour in use. It also makes the continuing work visible instead of treating durable systems as self-sustaining.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Tools, roofs, paths, and cloth last through familiar rounds of attention. 'thiranu' names that ordinary upkeep before repair becomes urgent.

semantic_domains.creation: care that keeps a made thing fit for its purpose
semantic_domains.physical: preservation of an object's condition or function through work
